evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Design and maintain robust Power BI models; ensuring scalable, high-performance systems with strong governance; Attention to data accuracy and quality
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Quality, accuracy, and governance dominate the JD framing, with emphasis on robust, high-performance systems and careful data stewardship.
evidence.domain_risk: Financial reporting and metrics; budgeting and forecasting; margin analysis; variance reporting; discretion handling confidential data
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Role directly impacts financial decision-making with high-risk consequences—finance teams rely on accuracy for P&L, forecasting, and audit.
evidence.data_team_maturity: Collaborate with BI teams to integrate financial and operational data; Define standardized financial metrics; Partner with finance and operational leaders
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Data and BI functions exist with named teams (BI teams, finance, operations), suggesting mid-stage development with established roles.
evidence.jd_authorship: Design and maintain robust Power BI models; Build advanced DAX measures for margin analysis, KPIs, trends, and variance reporting; Enhance finance processes through automation
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Mix of specific technical responsibilities (DAX, margin analysis, variance reporting) with generic collaboration language ('Partner with finance and operational leaders').
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Support budgeting and forecasting; Define standardized financial metrics; Partner with finance and operational leaders; margin analysis
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Primary focus is financial reporting, FP&A, and budgeting—candidate serves finance team needs for decision-making.
evidence.autonomy_level: Design and maintain robust Power BI models; Build advanced DAX measures; Define standardized financial metrics; Partner with finance and operational leaders
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Role owns data model architecture and metric definition but executes in service of finance team priorities, creating mixed technical autonomy with business execution.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: No mention of AI tools, ML, or AI-related candidate expectations in the JD.
evidence.testing_framing: Attention to data accuracy and quality
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Data quality appears as a skill requirement and mindset expectation, not as an owned testing responsibility or framework.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Attention to data accuracy and quality; Discretion handling confidential data; strong governance; financial reporting; variance reporting
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: High emphasis on accuracy, confidentiality, and governance reflects risk of bad financial data reaching decision-makers or violating regulatory requirements.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Enhance finance processes through automation; reduce spreadsheet reliance; Collaborate with BI teams to integrate; improve manual/Excel-based processes
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Dominant verbs focus on improving and automating existing processes rather than building new systems from zero.
evidence.loss_aversion: Discretion handling confidential data; strong governance; Attention to data accuracy and quality. Role emphasizes preventing bad financial data and maintaining information security.
evidence.work_arrangement: This hybrid role focuses on transforming the way the business uses data
